tested_outcome: Production-ready first pass. Condenser used as-is properly, hierarchy clean, $58/week dominant, 'EASIER THAN YOU THINK' accent did the curiosity-gap work.

input_context.headline: PERTH HOMEOWNERS — DUCTED A/C IS EASIER THAN YOU THINK
input_context.headline_accent_phrase: EASIER THAN YOU THINK (set in contrasting colour for curiosity gap)
input_context.value_stack: 10kW MHI Ducted System | 7-Year Manufacturer Warranty | Locally Installed by Perth Experts | $0 Upfront
input_context.trust_pill: LOCALLY INSTALLED BY PERTH EXPERTS (with location-pin icon)
input_context.single_brand_positioning: MHI only — no bottom logo strip, brand established via sub-headline

assets_attached.instruction: Use AS-IS, do not modify shape/fans/branding/proportions

composition_choices_that_worked: Geographic targeting unmissable — 'PERTH HOMEOWNERS' top banner + 'LOCALLY INSTALLED BY PERTH EXPERTS' bottom pill
Headline curiosity gap: 'EASIER THAN YOU THINK' as accent colour — counter-frames common objection that ducted is complex/disruptive
Single-brand positioning is CLEANER than multi-brand for premium feel — no logo strip, brand named in sub-headline + value stack
Condenser as locked component on right ~40% canvas width — AI added subtle orange backlight glow to integrate without modifying product
Price block: 'FROM' / oversized $58 / '/WEEK' in accent colour / 'FULLY INSTALLED' — communicates affordability + completeness
Aggressive black/orange palette emerged from AI — direct-response without looking like Harvey Norman flyer
key_pattern: Locked product hero (condenser) + AI-generated context around it. Same pattern works for any product hero (controllers, vents, etc.) — upload the real product, brief AI to compose around it, never let AI generate the product itself.
full_prompt_used:
You are being given two attached images:

IMAGE 1 (reference ad): Use this as a strong style and layout reference. Match its visual energy, density, aggressive direct-response feel, and overall composition — but DO NOT copy its headline, city, brand, or specific colours. Generate fresh ones per the brief below.

IMAGE 2 (condenser product shot): Use this image AS-IS for the condenser hero. Place it in the ad, do not modify the unit's shape, fan count, branding, or proportions. Recolour or relight only if needed to match the ad's palette — but the unit's structure must remain identical to the source.

Create a square 1080×1080 social media ad for a residential ducted air conditioning installer in Perth, Australia.

FUNNEL CONTEXT
This ad is for a hot/warm audience — buyers who already know what ducted A/C is and are deciding between providers. The job is to push them past the line with a clear local offer and aggressive pricing. No explainer work needed.

MOOD & STYLE
Aggressive, direct-response, unmissable. Choose a fresh dark high-contrast palette of your own with one strong accent colour for the offer elements. Bold display typography for the headline. All text must be readable at thumbnail size. This should feel like a volume-driver retail ad — strong, bold, slightly loud, but professional.

LAYOUT (top to bottom)

1. TOP BANNER STRIP
Small uppercase caps across the very top: "PERTH HOMEOWNERS"
Thin strip with subtle accent line beneath.

2. HEADLINE — upper third
Oversized bold uppercase: "DUCTED A/C IS EASIER THAN YOU THINK"
Stack across two or three lines. "EASIER THAN YOU THINK" set in contrasting accent colour. Dominant element.

3. SUB-HEADLINE
"10kW Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Ducted System" medium-weight uppercase.

4. HERO VISUAL — centre/right, roughly 40% of canvas width
Place the attached condenser product shot as hero. Right side, ~40% canvas width. Use AS PROVIDED — same shape, fan configuration, proportions. May add soft glow/backdrop for palette integration, do not modify unit itself.

5. PRICE BLOCK — left side, mid canvas
Stacked left-aligned text:
   - Small uppercase caps: "FROM"
   - Oversized bold dominant: "$58"
   - Medium accent colour: "/WEEK"
   - Beneath: small caps: "FULLY INSTALLED"

6. VALUE STACK — beneath price block, left side
Four tick-bullets:
   ✓ 10kW MHI Ducted System
   ✓ 7-Year Manufacturer Warranty
   ✓ Locally Installed by Perth Experts
   ✓ $0 Upfront

7. CTA BUTTON — bottom-left
Rounded pill: "LOCK IN YOUR INSTALL"

8. BADGE — bottom-right
Bold pill/burst with $ icon: "ZERO UPFRONT"

9. LOCATION TRUST PILL — bottom area
Small pill with location-pin icon: "LOCALLY INSTALLED BY PERTH EXPERTS"

HARD RULES
- 1080×1080 square
- All text readable at thumbnail size
- Condenser used AS PROVIDED — no fan changes, no brand mark changes, no re-rendering
- Do NOT include "0% interest" or "interest-free"
- Do NOT add people, faces, hands
- Do NOT add review badges
- Single-brand ad — NO multi-brand logo strip at bottom
- "PERTH" must appear in the top banner
lessons_for_layer_2: Locked component pattern: tell AI what to use AS-IS upfront, what's permitted to change (recolour for palette) and what isn't (shape/proportions/branding)
Single-brand vs multi-brand is a meaningful positioning lever — single-brand feels premium, multi-brand feels retail
Curiosity-gap headline accent ('EASIER THAN YOU THINK') counter-frames objections — useful pattern for any cold/warm transition ad
Geographic anchoring belongs in 3 places minimum: top banner, value stack ('Locally installed by X'), bottom trust pill — Meta uses geo-targeting + the ad must reinforce it
